hawkish_phrases: further increases in Bank Rate may be required for a sustainable return of inflation to target
if the outlook suggests more persistent inflationary pressures, it will respond forcefully, as necessary
there have been continuing signs of firmer inflation in domestic prices and wages that could indicate greater persistence
a further, more forceful response from monetary policy was justified
dovish_phrases: GDP is projected to continue to fall throughout 2023 and 2024 H1
CPI inflation is projected to fall sharply to some way below the 2% target in two years' time
GDP growth had been slowing and was now in negative territory
real wages had fallen through the course of the year
most of the tightening in policy over the past year was yet to feed through to the real economy
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to increase Bank Rate by 75 basis points to 3%, with the majority signaling further increases may be required albeit to a peak lower than market pricing, emphasizing persistent domestic inflation pressures despite projecting a prolonged recession.
